behavior_summary: Provides examine ('U8P_BASESCRL::look') and activate ('use') behavior for in-game scroll items. When a player inspects a scroll, the script reads the scroll's subtype (quality) and makes the item 'bark' a human-readable name/description for that subtype (e.g., "scroll of healing", "scroll of invisibility", "scroll of trap detection").

When a player uses (activates) a scroll, the script reads the same quality code and spawns the corresponding scroll-effect process/script (spawn(script_id, pid, item)), selecting the effect script by quality with a switch-like chain of comparisons. Unknown/unspecified quality values fall back to a generic/default branch.
The code uses temporary string allocation/free helpers to prepare messages for Item::bark, and it follows the engine's imply/suspend or yield pattern after performing bark/spawn calls.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BASESCRL::look: calls Item::getQuality(item) and then branches on the returned small integer to select a specific literal string to pass to Item::bark(item, string). If the code does not match a known case, it uses a generic 'scroll' label.

U8P_BASESCRL::use: calls Item::getQuality(item) and uses a switch-like chain to choose which external script to spawn; many discrete quality values map to distinct spawn targets (including ranges 0x00..0x0F and specific higher codes 0x32..0x37).
Both handlers allocate temporary C-strings for presentation, free those temporaries after the bark call, and then yield/suspend (engine scheduling) after spawn or bark return handling.
open_questions: Exact numeric-to-effect mapping for every quality code in the 0x00..0x0F range: some codes are listed in the disassembly, but a complete enumerated table is not available in the extracted snippet.
Precise calling-convention and stack layout for the pid/player parameter: pid is strongly inferred but not explicitly named in the disassembly metadata.
Whether any other global game state or quest flags are modified by specific scroll effects spawned by BASESCRL::use (those effects run in separate scripts and are not visible here).
Capitalization/punctuation and localized text variants of the strings presented by Item::bark (extracted literals represent one locale/compile).

main_function.name: U8P_BASESCRL::look
main_function.purpose: When the player inspects/examines a scroll item, determine the scroll's subtype by calling Item::getQuality(item) and present a user-visible descriptive string via Item::bark. Known quality values map to specific descriptive strings; otherwise a generic 'scroll' label is shown.
entry_conditions: Triggered by an examine/U8P_BASESCRL::look event (player or actor looks at the scroll).
Runs with the item context available and with an implicit pid/player process id pushed for bark.

function_map.name: U8P_BASESCRL::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_BASESCRL::look handler: read item quality and display the appropriate name/description via Item::bark.

function_map.name: U8P_BASESCRL::use
function_map.role: Use/activate handler: read item quality and spawn the corresponding scroll-effect script, passing pid and item.

function_map.name: Item::getQuality
function_map.role: Engine/library call that returns a small integer quality/subtype code for a given Item instance; used to determine behavior in both U8P_BASESCRL::look and use.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/library call that displays text or makes an item 'speak' to a player/process; used by U8P_BASESCRL::look to show the scroll name/description.

function_map.name: spawn
function_map.role: Engine/system call to create/launch a new process/script for the scroll's effect; used by use to start effect handlers.

function_map.name: U8P_BASESCRL::string helpers (allocate/strToPtr/free)
function_map.role: Helpers used to create temporary C-string pointers from literals and free them after use for Item::bark argument preparation.

external_calls.purpose: Obtain the scroll's subtype/quality code to decide which descriptive string to display.
Present a human-readable name/description of the scroll (the string chosen based on quality).
Allocate/prepare a temporary C-string for Item::bark and free it afterward.
Read the scroll's subtype code to determine which effect script to spawn.
Launch the scroll-effect process/script corresponding to the quality code, passing pid and item as arguments.
Potentially prepare strings for any messages generated during use (less commonly observed than in U8P_BASESCRL::look).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides examine ('U8P_BASESCRL::look') and activate ('use') behavior for in-game scroll items. When a player inspects a scroll, the script reads the scroll's subtype (quality) and makes the item 'bark' a human-readable name/description for that subtype (e.g., "scroll of healing", "scroll of invisibility", "scroll of trap detection").
